name: Good Enough Practices in Scientific Computing
description: A set of practical computing habits every researcher can adopt — covering data management, code organization, collaboration, version control, and project structure.
author: The Carpentries Lab
prerequisiteNotes: No specific prerequisites. Basic coding familiarity is helpful for the Code episode.
learningObjectives:
After this lesson, the learner should be able to:
- Apply data management practices that support reproducibility and reuse
- Organize code and software for clarity and maintainability
- Structure research projects to support collaboration
- Use version control to track changes across a project
- Identify practical next steps for improving research computing workflows
abstract: A peer-reviewed Carpentries Lab lesson drawing on published sources and over a decade of workshop experience. Covers the core habits researchers need to work more efficiently and reproducibly: data management, code organization, project structure, collaboration, version control, and manuscript preparation.
audience: Researchers, Graduate Students, Scientists, Research Staff
notes: Peer-reviewed and stable. Broad audience — suitable as an early entry point for researchers new to computational practices.
teaches: Apply good enough computing practices to organize data, code, and projects for reproducible scientific research.
